name: storage
context: Create Azure Storage accounts and move artifacts through blob containers with `az storage` commands
info: Azure Storage is the default artifact and data substrate for many Azure automation paths. Agents use this skill to create storage accounts, create blob containers, upload and download zip artifacts or other files, list blobs, and hand connection strings to legacy consumers when login-based auth is not available. The core execution surface is `az storage account`, `az storage container`, and `az storage blob`.
triggers: upload a file to azure blob storage
manage azure blob storage from cli
body:
# Azure Storage

Most Azure deployment flows need storage somewhere: Function Apps need a storage account, build pipelines publish zip artifacts to blob storage, and applications often need object storage for uploads or generated files.

~~~bash
az storage account create \
  --name dojobuildstore \
  --resource-group rg-dojo \
  --location eastus \
  --sku Standard_LRS \
  --kind StorageV2

az storage container create \
  --name artifacts \
  --account-name dojobuildstore \
  --auth-mode login

az storage blob upload \
  --account-name dojobuildstore \
  --container-name artifacts \
  --name app.zip \
  --file ./dist/app.zip \
  --auth-mode login

az storage blob list --account-name dojobuildstore --container-name artifacts --auth-mode login --output table
az storage blob download --account-name dojobuildstore --container-name artifacts --name app.zip --file ./app.zip --auth-mode login
~~~

Prefer `--auth-mode login` when the executing identity already has RBAC on the storage account. Use connection strings only when the downstream tool cannot authenticate with Entra-backed login. The CLI can still surface a connection string with `az storage account show-connection-string`, but that should be treated as a compatibility path rather than the default.

This skill is the usual hand-off point before [[azure/functions]] zip deploys and a common dependency for [[azure/app-service]] build artifacts or user-upload features.

sections.title: Storage account creation
sections.body: `az storage account create` provisions the account namespace and redundancy tier. `StorageV2` is the normal general-purpose choice. Pick the resource group and region first, then settle redundancy and network posture later if the workload requires more than the default path.

sections.title: Login auth versus connection strings
sections.body: `az storage` commands accept `--auth-mode login` or a `--connection-string`. Login mode is better for agents that already have RBAC and should avoid long-lived account keys. Connection strings are still useful when feeding older tooling or SDKs that cannot use the current CLI identity directly.

sections.title: Blob upload, list, and download
sections.body: `az storage blob upload`, `list`, and `download` are the day-to-day artifact operations. Use upload for explicit one-file transfers, `upload-batch` when publishing a whole directory, and list before destructive cleanup or deployment promotion so the agent can confirm the exact blob names present.

links.context: Use login-based auth or switch subscriptions before storage operations
Create the resource group before the storage account
Function Apps depend on storage accounts and often deploy zip artifacts from them
Web apps often consume zip artifacts or uploaded content stored here
Store connection strings only when login-based auth is not viable

related.note: Storage accounts are usually created inside a known workload group
S3 is the closest object-storage analogue elsewhere in the graph
R2 is another object-storage target for artifact or file workflows

scripts.name: Manage Storage
scripts.description: Create storage accounts, create containers, and transfer blobs with Azure CLI

AZURE_STORAGE_ACTION.description: One of account-create, container-create, blob-upload, blob-download, blob-list, or show-connection-string

AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP.description: Resource group for account creation or connection string lookup

AZURE_LOCATION.description: Azure region for storage account creation

AZURE_STORAGE_SKU.description: Storage SKU such as Standard_LRS or Standard_ZRS

AZURE_STORAGE_BLOB_NAME.description: Blob name, defaults to the source file basename on upload

AZURE_STORAGE_AUTH_MODE.description: Either login or key; ignored when connection string is supplied

AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING.description: Optional connection string for legacy or key-based auth

AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: Subscription ID or name to activate before the command

id.description: ARM resource ID or storage result identifier

primaryEndpoints.description: Storage service endpoints when creating an account

depends.reason: Login-based storage auth requires an Azure principal with RBAC on the account
Storage accounts are generally created inside a resource group
The executable surface is a Bash wrapper around `az storage` commands

author: dojo-community
